summary: L108 shows two scorer blind spots: a generic refusal cue can satisfy a refusal field too easily, and mixed answers need a case-level abstention rule that preserves an answer when at least one required answer field passes. I propose field-specific refusal fixtures plus a mixed-answer abstention override, with no direct code edits.

accepted_fields_or_changes: Add fixture-level refusal evidence for refusal fields, so pass/fail does not rely on a generic global refusal marker alone. For q002-style mixed answers, a refusal field should pass only when the response contains the field-specific refusal cue plus the field topic, not just any refusal phrase.

Extend the field fixture schema with an optional field such as refusal_markers or refusal_evidence, then use that for expected_action in {refuse, withhold} before falling back to generic refusal detection.

Treat mixed answers as answered when any answer/answer_with_caveat field passes, even if a refusal field is only partially satisfied. This keeps a supported answer from being counted as whole-answer abstention.
Record a mixed-answer diagnostic flag, e.g. mixed_supported_answer_and_refusal, so the evaluator can report the split without collapsing it into a pure abstention or pure pass.
rejected_or_risky_items: Do not let the global REFUSAL_MARKERS list by itself certify a refusal field.
Do not require all fields to pass before clearing abstention if the case already contains a satisfied answer field.
Do not expand the change into EDD scoring; keep it diagnostic and fixture-driven.
needs_orchestrator_review: Confirm whether the mixed-answer override should affect only the abstention label or also the downstream field_score case_passed summary.
Confirm the new fixture key name for refusal-specific evidence before implementation.

observed_results: field_level_contact_scorer.py currently passes refusal fields when has_refusal_marker is present and the required marker is found, even if the refusal cue is generic rather than field-specific.
evaluate.py currently derives abstention from is_abstention(answer) and only conditionally clears it from field_score, which makes mixed answers sensitive to how case_passed is computed.

The L108 fixture set includes an explicit mixed answer/refusal example at q20260707_l108_002 and a control set where answerable fields and refusal fields are separated, which is enough to test a stricter mixed-answer rule.
The baseline details show false_abstention_rate at 0.6, so the current behavior already has enough signal to justify a narrow diagnostic change.
